channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Issues Executive Orders to Tackle Housing Supply, Demand
item.description: The orders could undermine bipartisan legislation the Senate passed earlier this week, the most significant housing package in decades.
item.dc:creator: Ronda Kaysen
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Home prices are about 50 percent higher than they were at the start of the pandemic, and rents were up about 17 percent in December 2025 from December 2019, making housing affordability a primary concern of many voters.

item.title: With Endorsement, Trump Clears Oklahoma Senate Path
item.description: The move, as other Republicans confirmed they would not run, all but assured Representative Kevin Hern would succeed Senator Markwayne Mullin.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump endorsed Representative Kevin Hern in a social media post on Friday night and called him “a true friend of MAGA.”

item.title: Pentagon’s Boat Bombings Are Illegal, Human Rights Panel Is Told
item.description: Experts condemned the U.S. campaign at a human rights meeting in Guatemala, and the Trump administration condemned the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Moises Castillo/Associated Press
item.media:description: Jamil Dakwar of the A.C.L.U. said the Pentagon’s attacks were being undertaken without the authorization of Congress and were “in violation of international law on the use of force.”

item.title: Trump’s War Alliance With Israel Is Reshaping the Middle East. But It Carries Risks.
item.description: President Trump is the first American leader to embrace fighting a full-fledged, joint war with Israel. Washington has tried to avoid that level of coordination in the past.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at Mar-a-Lago in December.

item.title: The Michigan Synagogue Attacker Was a Quiet Restaurant Worker
item.description: Days before the antisemitic violence, an imam recalled seeing Ayman Mohamad Ghazali at a service for his relatives who had been killed in the war in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Kurt Streeter, Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs, Hwaida Saad and Neal E. Boudette
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, who officials said was killed after driving a car into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township, Mich., had stopped showing up to work in the days before the attack.

item.title: Temple Israel Staff Raced to Save Lives in Michigan Synagogue Attack
item.description: “I didn’t breathe until I knew all of the kids were OK,” one teacher recalled after a truck crashed through the doors of a Michigan synagogue this week.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman and Katherine Rosman
item.media:credit: ABC Affiliate WXYZ, via Reuters
item.media:description: Smoke rising from Temple Israel in West Bloomfield, Mich., on Thursday.

item.title: Judge Blocks Trump Order Ending Deportation Protections for Somalis, for Now
item.description: The judge said she was staying the order indefinitely in large part because the Trump administration had been unprepared to defend it in court.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Karmel Mall, a Somali-owned shopping center in Minneapolis, in January. The city was the focus of an aggressive immigration crackdown in Minnesota, the state with the largest population of Somalis.

item.title: Gunman in Old Dominion Shooting Appeared to Target ROTC, Court Records Show
item.description: Before opening fire in a university classroom, the gunman asked people if they were there for a Reserve Officers Training Corps event, court documents said.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: John Clark/Associated Press
item.media:description: Law enforcement officers investigating the shooting at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Va., on Thursday.

item.title: Before Deadly Old Dominion Shooting, Parallel Lives of 2 Soldiers Diverged
item.description: Lt. Col. Brandon Shah flew hundreds of combat missions as a war-zone helicopter pilot before being shot and killed in his Old Dominion University classroom by a radicalized military veteran.
item.dc:creator: Dave Philipps
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Allen G. Breed/Associated Press
item.media:description: The authorities on Thursday investigated the scene at Constant Hall at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Va., where the shooting occurred.

item.title: Judge Temporarily Blocks Trump Demand for Student Race Data
item.description: A group of Democratic attorneys general had sued to overturn the Trump administration’s new policy that demanded the past seven years of student application data.
item.dc:creator: Michael C. Bender and Vimal Patel
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: Linda McMahon, the education secretary, at a briefing at the White House last year.

item.title: Chemical Smell at Control Center Halts Traffic at Washington and Baltimore Airports
item.description: The ground delays, which also affected the airport serving Richmond, Va., were expected to last until at least midnight, according to the F.A.A., which said the smell had affected air traffic controllers.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk
item.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport was among the airports where the F.A.A. halted flights on Friday afternoon.

item.title: Charges Dropped Against Teenagers Whose Teacher Died in Prank Gone Wrong
item.description: The teacher’s relatives said they supported “getting the charges dropped for all involved” after a student prank led to his death last week in Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

item.title: Justice Dept. Drops Prosecution of Veteran Who Burned American Flag
item.description: The veteran, Jay Carey, was arrested the same day that President Trump signed an executive order to punish flag burning, a First Amendment right.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:credit: Rod Lamkey, Jr./Associated Press
item.media:description: Jay Carey with supporters after his arraignment in Washington in September. Mr. Carey had not been charged with flag burning itself but with two misdemeanors related to setting a fire on federal land.

item.title: Marines Issue Findings on California Shrapnel Incident
item.description: The October episode sent shrapnel onto California Highway Patrol vehicles on Interstate 5 during a celebratory exercise conducted for Vice President JD Vance and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Laurel Rosenhall
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Mario Tama/Getty Images
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance, left, watches the Marines conduct an amphibious exercise at Camp Pendleton’s Red Beach in October. During the event, shrapnel went flying onto a California Highway Patrol vehicle.

item.title: What to Know About AI Political Campaign Ads During Election Season
item.description: Wait, that’s not the real James Talarico …
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: The real James Talarico. Republicans recently released a video with an A.I.-generated version of him reading his old tweets.

item.title: Protesters Accused of Antifa Ties Found Guilty of Support for Terrorism
item.description: The guilty verdicts marked the first time that terrorism charges had been successfully brought against purported members of antifa.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Krista Torralva
item.media:credit: Louis DeLuca/The Dallas Morning News, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The Prairieland Detention Center in Alvarado, Texas.

item.title: Judge Orders V.A. to Restore Union Contract With 300,000 Workers
item.description: Doug Collins, the V.A. secretary, had moved to nullify the contract in August, after Trump sought to eliminate collective-bargaining rights for federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
item.media:description: Doug Collins, the secretary of veterans affairs, on Capitol Hill last year.

item.title: Judge Quashes Justice Dept.’s Subpoenas of Fed, Crippling Its Pursuit of Trump’s Rivals
item.description: Judge James E. Boasberg derided the U.S. attorney’s office in Washington for pursuing a case against Jerome H. Powell that appeared to be motivated by President Trump’s desire for vengeance.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer, Glenn Thrush and Colby Smith
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: “The government has offered no evidence whatsoever that Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve chair, committed any crime other than displeasing the president,” Judge James E. Boasberg wrote.

item.title: Family Members of Michigan Synagogue Attacker Died in Airstrike in Lebanon
item.description: The man, a U.S. citizen born in Lebanon, lost family members during an airstrike there last week. The attack on a Michigan synagogue rattled Jewish communities across America.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin, Hwaida Saad, Neal E. Boudette and Kurt Streeter
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township, Mich., on Friday.

item.title: Trump’s Move to Seize Oil Tankers Costs the U.S. Tens of Millions of Dollars
item.description: Although President Trump said seizing tankers would be a financial boon, the cost of maintaining just one aging ship has already reached $47 million.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Nicholas Nehamas
item.media:credit: Vantor, via Reuters
item.media:description: The oil tanker Skipper, seized by the United States, seen off the coast of Texas near Galveston in December.

item.title: Sam Page Poised to Beat Phil Berger in High Stakes N.C. Primary
item.description: A formal tally on Friday showed Sam Page ahead of Phil Berger, the powerful longtime leader of the State Senate. A recount is expected but experts say that is unlikely to flip the results.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina
item.media:credit: Cornell Watson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sam Page, a Republican candidate for North Carolina State Senate and a small-town sheriff.

item.title: Hegseth’s Claim About Killing a Would-Be Assassin Creates a Mystery
item.description: The defense secretary said the leader of an Iranian unit that had planned to assassinate President Trump had been killed. But U.S. officials privately acknowledge the story is not that simple.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Aaron Boxerman and Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth at the White House. “Iran tried to kill President Trump, and President Trump got the last laugh,” he said at a news conference last week.

item.title: Airport Security Workers to Miss Paycheck as Shutdown Drags On
item.description: Union officials warned that airport delays could worsen just as spring break season kicks in. Workers are taking on other jobs and canceling child care to make ends meet.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian and Madeleine Ngo
item.media:credit: Jeff Chiu/Associated Press
item.media:description: Transportation Security Administration workers at San Francisco International Airport last month.

item.title: Trump-Iran Timeline: Key Moments Leading Up to War
item.description: President Trump’s aggressive stance toward Iran, during his first term and since returning to office last year, is key to understanding the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald Trump speaks to members of the media alongside Steve Witkoff, special envoy, and Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth on board Air Force One last week.

item.title: Homes in This California Enclave Come With a Catch: Living on a Landslide Complex
item.description: The soil keeps shifting in Rancho Palos Verdes, Calif. For the right buyer, that presents a great opportunity, at least for the time being.
item.dc:creator: Mimi Dwyer

item.title: Welcome to the Neighborhood. It’s Sinking.
item.description: Portuguese Bend in Rancho Palos Verdes, Calif., rests on an active landslide. While some residents have fled, a few new homebuyers are choosing to take a risk and purchase anyway, seeing a rare opportunity to own a piece of Southern California coastal property.
item.dc:creator: Mimi Dwyer, Jeremy Raff, Yasu Tsuji and Nicholas Kraus

item.title: Wall Street Bankers Offered Lucrative Access to Join the Pentagon
item.description: A presentation from a headhunting firm aimed to recruit Wall Street investors to the Pentagon by offering “unmatched access” to government officials and fund-raising opportunities among foreign sovereigns.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The Pentagon recruiting effort offers Wall Street investors the opportunity to manage “more capital than most investors deploy in their entire careers.”

item.title: How to Vote in the 2026 Illinois Primary on Tuesday
item.description: Democratic and Republican candidates are vying for open seats in the U.S. Senate and House, and also for governor and other statewide offices.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson
item.media:credit: Nam Y. Huh/Associated Press
item.media:description: Attention on Tuesday in Illinois will largely focus on the Democratic primary to fill the open Senate seat long held by Senator Richard J. Durbin.

item.title: Hegseth Vows Lethal Day in Iran as Air War Intensifies
item.description: At a news conference, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth gave no indication of how long it would take before the Navy could escort civilian cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: “With every passing hour, we know, and we know they know, that the military capabilities of their evil regime are crumbling,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said.

item.title: War Stirs Mixed Feelings for the Only Iranian American Democrat in Congress
item.description: The daughter of Iranians who fled the country, Representative Yassamin Ansari of Arizona wants a democratic and secular government for Iran, but is wary of President Trump’s war.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Yassamin Ansari, Democrat of Arizona, is the only Democrat in Congress of Iranian heritage.

item.title: After Synagogue Attack in Michigan, Some Jews Wonder How Much More Security Is Possible
item.description: “We are synagogues — we are houses of worship,” one rabbi said. “We are not Fort Knox.”
item.dc:creator: Ruth Graham and Elizabeth Dias
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
item.media:description: A gunman killed 11 people at the Tree of Life synagogue in 2018.

item.title: Joaquin Castro Is on a Quest to Get Detained Immigrants Released
item.description: The Texas Democrat has used his perch in Congress to highlight sympathetic cases in his push to free detainees and call attention to the consequences of President Trump’s immigration agenda.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni and Tamir Kalifa

item.title: Hegseth’s Boasts of ‘Maximum’ Engagement Authorities Face Scrutiny After School Is Hit
item.description: The defense secretary has disparaged restrictive rules for opening fire that are aimed at reducing the risk of mistakes and civilian casualties.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: “The dumb, politically correct wars of the past were the opposite of what we’re doing here,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said last week.

item.title: ‘A Lot of Life Years Lost’: How NAFTA Shortened American Life Spans
item.description: A study tracks how the North American Free Trade Agreement and trade competition with Mexico led to earlier deaths for American factory workers.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: A steel mill in Braddock, Pa. Researchers found that the North American Free Trade Agreement sped up the decline of the U.S. manufacturing industry.

item.title: His Harvard Lab Was Thriving. Then Came the Cuts.
item.description: Will Mair, who studies aging, lost almost all his research funds when the White House cracked down on Harvard. He was wholly unprepared for the upheaval that followed.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.media:credit: Tony Luong for The New York Times
item.media:description: Will Mair is a Harvard University professor who studies aging.
